title: file put(): non-atomic write destroys secret on partial failure
description: Trigger: put() called when disk is full or I/O error occurs mid-write. Behavior: open() with O_TRUNC empties the file before write_all(); if write_all() then fails, the file is left empty (0 bytes). Next get() returns a 0-byte secret or NotFound (if file was created). The original secret value is irrecoverably lost. Fix: write to a temp file on the same filesystem, then atomic rename(). On POSIX, rename() is atomic when src and dst are on the same filesystem.
